channel.title: AP Physics C Knowledge Graph
channel.description: Last 10 notes on AP Physics C Knowledge Graph

item.title: Maxwell's Equations
item.description: Maxwell’s Equations Maxwell’s Equations are a set of four fundamental equations that describe all classical electromagnetic phenomena.

item.title: LC Circuits
item.description: LC Circuits An LC circuit contains an inductor and capacitor connected together, creating an oscillating system.

item.title: Inductance
item.description: Inductance Inductance is the property of an electrical conductor that opposes changes in current.

item.title: Faraday's Law
item.description: Faraday’s Law Faraday’s Law describes how a changing magnetic flux induces an electromotive force (emf).

item.title: Magnetic Flux
item.description: Magnetic Flux Magnetic flux \Phi_B measures the amount of magnetic field passing through a surface.

item.title: Magnetic Moments
item.description: Magnetic Moments The magnetic moment is a vector quantity that measures the strength and orientation of a magnetic source.

item.title: Magnetic Force on Current-Carrying Wires
item.description: Magnetic Force on Current-Carrying Wires A current-carrying wire in a magnetic field experiences a force due to the magnetic forces on the moving charges within the wire.

item.title: Magnetic Force on Moving Charges
item.description: Magnetic Force on Moving Charges A charged particle moving in a magnetic field experiences a force perpendicular to both its velocity and the magnetic field.

item.title: Ampère's Law
item.description: Ampère’s Law Ampère’s Law relates the magnetic field around a closed loop to the current passing through the loop.

item.title: Magnetic Field
item.description: Magnetic Field The magnetic field \vec{B} is a vector field that describes the magnetic influence on moving charges and magnetic dipoles.
